text.title: JS/TS Frontend Testing Failure Modes
text.summary: 5 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Visual style refactor renamed .btn to .button; 80 tests fail with no behaviour change.
antipattern.root-cause: Tests queried by CSS class instead of role.
antipattern.fix: Replace container.querySelector('.btn') with screen.getByRole('button', { name: /label/i }). If no semantic option exists, use data-testid with a comment.

antipattern.symptom: Vitest project has jest.fn(); CI errors with 'jest is not defined' or TypeScript error.
antipattern.root-cause: Author copied a Jest example into a Vitest project.
antipattern.fix: Use vi.fn(), vi.mock(), vi.spyOn() in Vitest. For Jest, the inverse. Pin the runner in the agent prompt.

antipattern.symptom: Snapshot files balloon to 500+ lines; PR reviews 'just update the snapshot' without inspecting the diff.
antipattern.root-cause: Default test generation emits toMatchSnapshot on the whole component.
antipattern.fix: Remove default snapshots; replace with targeted assertions (role/label/text). Reserve snapshots for serialisable data structures only.

antipattern.symptom: Manual QA finds that pressing Enter on the focused button does nothing in production; tests show 'click' works fine.
antipattern.root-cause: Tests use fireEvent.click; never simulate keyboard activation.
antipattern.fix: Switch to userEvent.click and userEvent.keyboard('{Enter}'); userEvent fires the realistic sequence.

antipattern.symptom: Accessibility audit finds 14 components with missing labels; tests passed for months.
antipattern.root-cause: No axe-core assertion in component tests; a11y caught only in manual audit.
antipattern.fix: Add one jest-axe / axe-core assertion per component test: const results = await axe(container); expect(results).toHaveNoViolations().
